mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in real activity-linked retail sales signals improving household demand and revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 433907.0 as of one month before 2014-04-15
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with positive household spending momentum
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in walk-forward tests, with Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening labor income and recession-risk repricing, causing investors to lower earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 6.7 as of one month before 2014-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if mean triggered return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains pinned near zero, real short rates fall and liquidity conditions support equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises above its prior 12-month pace
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.08 as of one month before 2014-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction state has a nonpositive average 60-day S&P 500 return or if the joint signal adds no return information beyond CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS separately.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates stronger factory output and operating leverage, raising near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses from negative to positive
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 101.6276 as of two months before 2014-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive crossings in INDPRO are not followed by above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts signals weaker construction activity and reduced demand for housing-linked goods, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 907.0 as of two months before 2014-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth paired with rising retail sales identifies labor-income transmission into consumption, improving revenue breadth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and RSAFS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 137928.0 as of one month before 2014-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS positive state does not outperform states where only one series is positive, or if its 20-day hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in the federal funds rate after a low-rate period raises discount rates and signals tighter liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.08 as of one month before 2014-04-15
regime_hint: start of a monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: Reject if federal-funds upward crossings produce nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the downside hit rate is below 50% in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with accelerating CPI identifies cost-pressure expansion, where input inflation compresses margins despite higher output and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises above its 12-month growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 235.64 as of one month before 2014-04-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up and CPI-accelerating state has positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if it does not underperform INDPRO-up-only states.
